response.justification: The role requires 8 years of C++ programming as a hard minimum, and the candidate shows only about 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments plus a university thesis project. The bulk of their career is full-stack web/backend development in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While there is genuine distributed-systems exposure (microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby, Kubernetes deployments) and informal mentoring/leadership through workshops and talks, the near-total absence of sustained C++ and cloud-compute-platform experience makes this a poor match for a C++-centric infrastructure technical lead role.
strengths: Distributed/high-throughput systems experience (microservices at millions of req/sec)
Long track record with mentoring, workshops, and engineering best practices (TDD/CI/CD)
Broad full-stack and infrastructure breadth across web, embedded, and DevOps
concerns: Far short of the required 8 years of C++ (only ~6 months plus a thesis)
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale C++ infrastructure ownership
No formal technical-leadership title managing cloud/DevOps engineers
